name: Mack Hollins
headline: Efficient field-stretcher in New England, but volume caps his fantasy ceiling
body:
Hollins quietly turned in his most productive efficiency season yet, posting a 0.418 EPA per play and a 63.1% success rate across 14 games while finishing as the WR54. His 13.5% target share and 0.331 WOPR landed him 65 looks for 46 grabs, 550 yards and 2 scores, and the underlying profile screams deep threat: 13.1 air yards per target, 18.5 air yards per reception and a 54-yard long. He dropped zero of 46 catchable balls and won 70.8% of his targets, so the hands and downfield juice are real.

The problem is opportunity. At 32 and slotted as a complementary piece in New England's pecking order, he projects for roughly 11 catches and 127 yards by consensus, with an ADP outside the top 280. Last year's red-zone usage was thin (5 targets, 2 TDs), and his best-ever mark of 9.1 PPR points per game underscores a floor-over-ceiling reality. He's a deep-league dart, nothing more.
